title: Cohort Retention Grid

landmines.name: month-offset-arithmetic
landmines.kills: Deriving the offset from the month number alone (ignoring the year) or from days/30 mislabels offsets across month ends, the leap day, and the year boundary -- e.g. Jan 2023 against a Jan 2022 signup collapses to offset 0 and the cross-year run is lost. Offset must be (year*12+month) on both dates. Kills naive/1_month_only_offset.sql.

landmines.name: dormant-denominator
landmines.kills: Cohort size counts every signup, including dormant customers who never order. Building the denominator from ordering customers only overstates every retention rate. Kills naive/2_dormant_excluded_denominator.sql.

landmines.name: distinct-customer-numerator
landmines.kills: The numerator is COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id); COUNT(*) of orders double counts a customer who orders more than once in a month, overstating retention (and it can exceed 1).

landmines.name: guest-not-a-cohort
landmines.kills: Orders with NULL customer_id (guests) belong to no cohort and must be excluded; the join to the cohort table drops them naturally, but a COALESCE into a NULL mega-cohort would wrongly count them.

landmines.name: correlated-per-cell-tle
landmines.kills: Filling each cohort/offset cell with correlated subqueries over ~228k orders is quadratic and exceeds the time limit; the reference computes every cell in one grouped pass. TLE for naive/3_correlated_per_cell_tle.sql.

notes: naive/3_correlated_per_cell_tle.sql is the designated TLE-slow solution: correct on small fixtures, times out at Black scale. The two WA naives fail on correctness at sample scale (offset arithmetic and denominator). Month parts are extracted via SUBSTR + CAST AS INTEGER; only populated cells (active_customers >= 1) are emitted.
